programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: The prosecution portrays Alex Murdaugh as a desperate thief on the verge of being exposed.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: The State argues that what happened because of allegations resulted in the double homicide.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: The 2019 deadly boat crash linked to this case involved the Murdaugh family's boat. Plus, Paul was accused of being under the influence while driving that boat.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: Jurors hear from the Murdaugh family's housekeeper who worked for them for more than a decade.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: A close housekeeper gives testimony on her decade-long friendship with Maggie Murdaugh.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: The lead prosecutor challenged the credibility of Susan Wright testifying for her own defense in her murder trial. During cross-examination, Kelly Siegler accused her of intentionally killing her husband, Jeff, and then covering up the crime. This fiery cross-examination challenged the defendant's claim that she had no choice to kill her allegedly abusive husband in their bedroom that night when he supposedly came at her with a knife.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: The lead prosecutor had more probing questions for her on the stand. The defendant admitted to lying everyone after she fatally stabbed her husband, Jeff. On the stand, Susan Wright gave a reason to the lies claiming the whole ordeal was a nightmare that she didn't fully comprehend. The defendant then claimed that after burying him in a makeshift grave in the backyard she claimed to still be afraid he was alive and would come after her.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: Susan Wright wraps up her emotional testimony to the jury. She emphasized to jurors that she was not lying. Lead prosecutor Kelly Siegler got animated with the defendant on the stand trying to get her to admit to her alleged wrongdoing. Prosecutors believed she tied her husband on the bed by luring him for sex. Once defenseless Wright stabbed him nearly 200 times to death. Next, the Defense brought in a crime scene reconstructionist as their last expert witness for jurors to hear.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: The last witness to testify wrapped up his testimony. He was the forensic scientist who evaluated the crime scene where Jeff Wright lost his life and concluded that there was no evidence of a premeditated murder. Later, jurors listened to closing arguments. Prosecutors began with their theory of a carefully planned out murder at home. This version of Jeff's death went against Susan's defense where they claimed that she feared for her life before stabbing her husband to death.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: "Follow the bloody trail" was the theme in the crime scene technician's testimony. Investigators found bloodstains in different parts of the home. The death investigation of Gary Farris, a prominent real estate attorney, was an evidence-heavy case. Crime scene investigators uncovered a trail of blood, bullet projectiles, and other key pieces of evidence linked to how Gary lost his life. The tedious task in processing a large crime scene was made clear to jurors.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: So far, crime scene photos have dominated this trial. Just hours after police showed up to the scene where Gary Farris' remains lay, crime scene technicians took numerous photos, flew drones, and processed key pieces of evidence. The prosecution team hoped all the crime scene photos would show evidence proving Melody killed her husband of 38 years. What started off with a hunch that he accidentally fell into a fire pit turned into details of a cover-up in a fatal shooting.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learned of the details of the burn pile where Gary Farris's charred remains were found. Investigators did their best to sift, excavate and collect all the identified human remains in that pile area. The human remains first dug up by police were presented in Court. One of the crime scene technicians claimed that the burn pit where the victim's remains were found was still smoldering. The lead crime scene investigator remembered how hot it was when he started working in the area.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: Melody Farris's lead attorney had more questions for the lead crime scene investigator testifying. Chris Shaw recalled working with his team to meticulously identify and collected the human remains of Gary found charred in a fire pit. The Defense nitpicked the lead crime scene investigators work in this case. Melody's defense accused police of confirmation bias in only zeroing in on her for the murder of her husband.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Huntington Beach, 1968. Boys on their way to school discover the brutalized body of a young woman in an oil field. Police are unable to identify her or her killer. It will take 52 years before DNA science is able to solve this double mystery.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1964 Hazleton, Pennsylvania, the brutalized body of 9-year-old Marise Chiverella is discovered after she disappears walking to school. Half a century later, a teen prodigy in investigative genetic genealogy helps detectives solve this crime.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Police work for decades with emerging forensic sciences to identify the killer of two women in Lake Tahoe.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: 40 years after the sexual assault and murder of 20-year-old Robin Brooks in Sacramento, California, determined cold case detectives collaborate with scientists using revolutionary forensic techniques to identify her killer and put him behind bars.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1992, the decapitated body of a young woman is discovered near the Arizona Canal in Phoenix. Ten months later, another body - this time a teen - is located nearby. Dedicated detectives use forensic science to catch the infamous killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy is used to resolve the 1988 murder of a mother killed in front of her toddler.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy reveals the killer of a young mother murdered in her own home 34 years prior.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A young woman is found beaten, raped and strangled in Garden Grove, California in 1987. Two years pass until a woman in a neighboring jurisdiction meets the same fate. It will take years before forensics and police identify this serial killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1990 Mississippi, a woman is kidnapped and raped at knifepoint. Police are unable to identify the perpetrator. It'll take forward-thinking investigators, state-of-the-art science and brave survivors to put this man behind bars.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1972 Washington, 20-year-old Jody Loomis is discovered covered in blood by the roadside. She is brought to the hospital where she passes away from a bullet wound. It'll take over forty years for science to reveal her killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In Willoughby, Ohio, Mark Madger comes home to find his wife Nadine lying in a pool of her own blood, stabbed to death. Four decades later, cutting-edge DNA science provides answers the Madger family have been waiting a lifetime for.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1985, a young woman is awoken by an intruder who sexually assaults her at gunpoint in her own condo. The determination of the survivor will lead her on a decades-long journey to justice through a revolutionary forensic science technique.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy unmasks the sexual deviant who raped and killed Christina Castiglione in 1983.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The 1972 rape and murder of 9-year-old Debbie Lynn Randall is solved thanks to new science - 50 years later.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Cold-case detectives try to solve the decades-long murder of Sharon Hammack using forensic technology.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: While jogging along a scenic canal, a young woman is brutally attacked and raped by a stranger. Police use groundbreaking genetic genealogy to identify the perpetrator.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 2010, Sherry Black is found stabbed to death in her bookstore in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police fail to identify her killer until advancements in DNA science reignite the case.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1976 Wisconsin, 25-year-old David Schuldes and 24-year-old Ellen Matheys are murdered in a park. The case goes cold until 2015 when advancements in DNA science reignite the investigation.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: When Clare Holman is murdered in 1994, her case quickly goes cold, and many assume it will stay that way. But twenty years later, new technology finally provides answers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, the FBI and the NYPD began a joint investigation into gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. However, they soon realized Dhinsa ruled his mob-like operation with brutal force, kidnapping and killing wayward employees.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: In 1964, civil rights workers who set out to educate Southern Black people about the right to vote disappeared in Mississippi. After they were found lynched, the FBI gained the trust of an informant who led to the conviction of local KKK members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Puerto Rican separatist group known as the FALN was suspected of setting off bombs in major U.S. cities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the FBI was overwhelmed with investigations in Seattle which had the third-highest number of bank robberies in the nation. Agents focused on a series of heists in which two heavily armed men seemed to know where the banks stored their money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children and police were left baffled. The crime seemed unmotivated and random. But family members hinted that the victim, who had been married to a prominent attorney, harbored a dark secret.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenage immigrant came to the United States to realize her dream of being a doctor but she found herself an innocent victim of drug trafficking. Agents discovered a trail of drugs, hit men and murder as they attempted to solve her kidnapping.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two robbers with assault rifles ambushed an armored car, killing both guards and seizing thousands of dollars.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1995, a mother of three was murdered and her corpse burned in Los Angeles, California. Agents hunted down a charismatic man named Glen Rogers who used charm and generosity to target women for his brutal attacks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A mother discovered her young son was missing from school. Within hours, the boy's entire family was frantically searching for him. But when the few leads quickly turned cold, the FBI was called in to assist in the investigation.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: This special episode examines previous cases to learn about the investigative techniques used by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June 1973, a young girl was abducted from a state park while camping with her family. A criminal profiler predicted that the kidnapper would contact the girl's family on the one-year anniversary of her abduction.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Richmond, Virginia, two masked men who were robbing a bank escaped after opening fire on officers responding to the call. Agents found one bank teller dead, another wounded; the bank's security guard had been shot four times yet was still alive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: For two couples on vacation, an island paradise turned to hell when one couple murders the other. Eleven years after the murder, a drifter sailing the high seas to escape a drug charge was convicted of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1987, Cuban prisoners at the Atlanta Federal Prison broke out homemade weapons and stole from prison staff.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young woman went missing from her home on a bright spring morning. After several days of searching, agents suspected a paroled criminal who frequented her health club may have abducted her.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Amid the cocaine wars of 1980s Miami, a multi-agency tactical squad suspected that a group of cops might be involved in illegal drug busts. The squad uncovered a ring of police who were making millions by ripping off smuggled cocaine.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Victor Farrant is a convicted rapist who murdered his former girlfriend in 1996 and attempted to murder another woman after he was released from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Lonnie David Franklin Jr. is better known by the nickname 'Grim Sleeper.' He lured vulnerable women into his car before murdering and sexually assaulting them for more than two decades.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the I-95 killer, who brutally killed six men in 1994 before being executed three years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Keith Jesperson, known as the Smiley Face Killer, killed eight women across several states in the 1990s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a killer so evil that not even her own son could escape her clutches. She became the first woman to be executed in Florida since 1848.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In March 2005, the gardaí recovered human body parts from the Royal Canal in Dublin. The victim's head was missing. He was identified as Farah Swaleh Noor, a Kenyan with a history of violence against women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: William appeared to be a friendly gardener, but his battered van hid a horrifying secret. It served as a mobile prison where he killed women before dumping their bodies in an isolated area he chillingly called his "garden."

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2007, single mother of three Paige Birgfeld went missing. Police in Grand Junction, Colorado investigating her disappearance discovered she'd been secretly working as an escort and suspected one of her clients of abducting and killing her.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Runge raped and killed 6 women and a young girl in Chicago, Illinois. The Police had suspected him in the early investigation but lacked the evidence to make an arrest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Carla, a bright and beloved teen, was abducted and murdered, shattering her community. For decades, the case remained unsolved until new investigative efforts finally pointed to Glen as the man responsible.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A master manipulator accused of the murder of his wife eventually caught out by a polygraph test - and his own fiancée.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Mikel Brady leads a violent prison escape attempt, killing four staff members before being recaptured.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Edward Johnson is sent to prison for the brutal murder of a female friend, but the violence doesn't end there.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Imprisoned after a botched robbery that ends with the fatal shooting of an innocent man, Craig Wissink later kills a corrections officer with a fire extinguisher in a brutal attack.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Jesse Con-ui's loyalty to the New Mexican Mafia drove him to betray and murder his former mentor.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In March 2005, the gardaí recovered human body parts from the Royal Canal in Dublin. The victim's head was missing. He was identified as Farah Swaleh Noor, a Kenyan with a history of violence against women.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of Philadelphia college athlete Aimee Willard is investigated after her car is found on the roadside. When her body is found, the police find unusual marks and DNA evidence that eventually lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating an apparent overdose suicide, detectives recover incriminating files from a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared along a California highway in 1991. Three years later, the case was solved when a man's refrigerated truck was looked upon with suspicion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1987 disappearance of Houston resident Tracy Jo Shine is recalled. The investigation went cold until 2000 when a "cold case squad" learned that the woman's ex-boyfriend Michael Neal had bragged about killing her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 62-year-old man opens a car restoration business and becomes seriously ill, his doctors cannot find the cause, but forensics can; identification of a highly toxic metal in the victim's blood leads to his poisoner.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After 6-year-old Michelle Lee Dorr went missing, her father confessed to murdering her. But investigators soon poked holes in his story, leaving the case unsolved for 14 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1996 investigation into 48-year-old Martha Hansen's murder is helped by a legal requirement that bars have video cameras installed on the premises.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the mail-order bride of a former army sergeant goes missing, investigators look into the deaths of the man's previous two wives.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott, police use a garbage bag to connect a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body. Finally, investigators use a single tooth found among some charred remains to identify her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The only clue recovered from a shooting is a twelve-guage shotgun. Scientists use unique methods to lift the serial number from the weapon to trace it to its owner

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist starts several fires that cause millions of dollars worth of damage and the deaths of three people. He is discovered, partly through the use of forensic hypnosis.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a serial killer was terrorizing the city of St. Louis, the FBI didn't need lab tests, fingerprints or any of the usual methods. All they needed was a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the case of the Center City Rapist and the murder of Shannon Schieber, Philadelphia authorities use an anonymous letter and geographic profiling to hone in on a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A three-year-old develops a respiratory condition. His father has Alzheimers. An investigator reveals that their ill health is due to mold in their modern home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder investigation in Florida crosses jurisdictions from New York and Jamaica. The police rely on cell phone mapping, wiretapping and a host of forensic evidence to link a suspect to a murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, the eyes of the world were on Virginia and Maryland. A serial sniper was on the loose, killing innocent, unsuspecting citizens. Just how was he caught?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman convicted of killing her mother in a car fire is granted a new trial. An expert witness has a different opinion as to how the fire started and, if proven, would clear the woman of any culpability.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Authorities track a kidnapper who let his victim write a last will and informed the family of her murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a history of heart disease. Rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, a 12-year-old was found murdered in her home in Minnesota. Despite a meticulous search of the home and an exhaustive investigation, police had no suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1962, the people of the small town of Hanford, California lost their sense of peace when one of their own, 15-year-old Marlene Miller, was murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two vanishes after a shopping trip. Her body is discovered a month later. Witnesses say they saw the victim being forced into a car by an unknown person.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is shot to death in her store just one day before she is to testify against the man accused of robbing her. The robber becomes the prime suspect, but he has a solid alibi.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the state of Florida was known for more than just Disney World. Worldwide attention focused on a rash of robberies which targeted tourists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: At a murder scene, investigators find a shoe-print, several foreign hairs, and unknown fingerprints. But they believe the most telling piece of evidence is the bite mark on the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman's death in Pennsylvania triggers a homicide investigation into another woman's death in North Carolina.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A nurse experiences a variety of flu-like symptoms. None of her doctors are able to discover the cause, until she visits the gynecologist for a routine check-up.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Time of death becomes pivotal after a pregnant woman is found murdered in her air-conditioned bedroom.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police were puzzled by an obscure print found at a crime scene where one man had been killed and two teenage girls were seriously injured.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man is found dead in his home, and his ex-wife has a perfect alibi. To determine time of death, investigators need to know when the victim ate his last meal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A union official is executed in his home not long after a strike by the union membership. Neither his wife nor anyone in the neighborhood heard any gunshots.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man tells police he shot an intruder who killed his wife. Forensic evidence disproves that story later on.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.
